# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Process

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_type:
        description: 'Type of release (e.g., major, minor, patch)'
        required: false
        default: 'patch'
      target_branch:
        description: 'Target branch for release (e.g., main, release/v1)'
        required: false
        default: 'main'
  push:
    branches:
      - main # Trigger on push to main for continuous release or specific branch

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write
  releases: write
  id-token: write # Required for OIDC authentication, e.g., publishing to some registries
  packages: write # Required if publishing to GitHub Packages
  pages: write # Required if deploying to GitHub Pages
  deployments: write # Required for creating deployments

jobs:
  build_and_publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      package_version: ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }}
      release_notes: ${{ steps.generate_notes.outputs.notes }} # Assuming release notes are generated here

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Needed for tools that inspect git history

      - name: Setup Node.js (example for JS projects)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org' # Example for npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Or yarn install, pip install, etc.

      - name: Build packages
        run: npm run build --if-present # Or mvn package, cargo build, etc.

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test --if-present # Or pytest, mvn test, etc.

      - name: Get package version
        id: get_version
        run: |
          # Example: Read version from package.json
          PACKAGE_VERSION=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")
          echo "version=$PACKAGE_VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Detected package version: $PACKAGE_VERSION"
        # For other languages:
        # Python: grep "__version__" my_package/__init__.py | cut -d "'" -f 2
        # Java: Use Maven/Gradle helper

      - name: Generate Release Notes (example)
        id: generate_notes
        run: |
          # This is a placeholder. A real implementation might use tools like
          # conventional-changelog, git-cliff, or GitHub's own release notes generator.
          # For simplicity, let's just make a dummy note.
          echo "notes=Automated release for version ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }}." >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Generated dummy release notes."

      - name: Publish packages
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} # Example for npm
          # GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # For GitHub Packages
        run: |
          # Example: npm publish
          # For a real project, ensure this only runs if the version hasn't been published
          # or if it's a new version.
          echo "Publishing package version ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }}..."
          # npm publish --access public
          echo "Package published (simulated)."

      - name: Archive build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist-files
          path: |
            ./dist/ # Example path for built files
            ./package.json # Include any other relevant files
          retention-days: 7

  create_github_release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build_and_publish
    if: success() # Only create release if build and publish succeeded

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist-files
          path: ./dist-artifacts/ # Download to a specific folder

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        id: create_release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.package_version }}
          name: Release v${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.package_version }}
          body: ${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.release_notes }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
          files: |
            ./dist-artifacts/* # Upload all downloaded artifacts as release assets
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # GITHUB_TOKEN has permissions due to workflow-level setting

  update_documentation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: create_github_release
    if: success() # Only update docs if release creation succeeded

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_branch || 'main' }} # Checkout target branch or main
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Use GITHUB_TOKEN for pushing changes

      - name: Setup environment (e.g., Python for Sphinx/MkDocs)
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'

      - name: Install documentation dependencies
        run: |
          pip install sphinx # Example: Install Sphinx
          # npm install typedoc # Example: Install TypeDoc for JS/TS projects

      - name: Generate documentation
        run: |
          # Example: Build Sphinx documentation
          # cd docs
          # make html
          # cd ..
          echo "Generating documentation for version ${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.package_version }}"
          echo "This is a dummy documentation update." > docs/latest_version.txt
          echo "Version: ${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.package_version }}" >> docs/latest_version.txt
          echo "Documentation updated at $(date)" >> docs/latest_version.txt
          mkdir -p docs/build # Example output directory
          echo "Documentation content for v${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.package_version }}" > docs/build/index.html

      - name: Configure Git for commits
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Commit and push documentation changes
        run: |
          git add docs/latest_version.txt docs/build/
          # Check if there are any changes to commit
          if git diff --cached --exit-code; then
            echo "No documentation changes to commit."
          else
            git commit -m "Docs: Update documentation for v${{ needs.build_and_publish.outputs.package_version }} [skip ci]"
            git push
            echo "Documentation changes committed and pushed."
          fi

      - name: Deploy documentation to GitHub Pages (optional)
        if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == format('refs/heads/{0}', github.event.inputs.target_branch || 'main')
        uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./docs/build # Directory containing the generated documentation
          # publish_branch: gh-pages # Default is gh-pages, can be main if using custom domain setup
          # cname: example.com # Optional: if using a custom domain
